results.title: The intersection of drug interactions and adverse reactions in contemporary antiretroviral therapy.
results.abstract: Advances in antiretroviral therapy (ART) have transformed HIV infection into a chronic and manageable condition. The introduction of potent and more tolerable antiretrovirals (ARVs) with favorable pharmacokinetic profiles has changed the prevalence and nature of drug-drug interactions (DDIs). Here, we review the relevance of DDIs in the era of contemporary ART.
results.journal: Current opinion in HIV and AIDS
mesh_terms: Drug-Related Side Effects and Adverse Reactions
